chapter_title.en: Chapter on Offspring, Milking, and Riding of Sacrificial Animals

text: 3086 - وَ سَأَلَ يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ شُعَيْبٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ أَ يَرْكَبُ هَدْيَهُ إِنِ اِحْتَاجَ إِلَيْهِ فَقَالَ «قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ «يَرْكَبُهَا غَيْرَ مُجْهِدٍ وَ لاَ مُتْعِبٍ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3086 - Ya'qub ibn Shu'ayb asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man—can he ride his hady (sacrificial animal) if he needs to?
Imam (as) replied: "The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said: 'He may ride it as long as he does not overburden or exhaust it.'"
